scenario: User wants to add a new skill to an existing plugin
expected_behavior: Skill loads plugin-validation and plugin-evaluation first to audit current state, records baseline eval pass rates, then guides through skill-foundry to write the new skill, wires it into plugin.json via plugin-composition, and runs regression check before declaring done

scenario: User has a plugin skill that activates on wrong queries
expected_behavior: Skill identifies this as an activation fix (low-medium risk), loads skill-foundry for frontmatter rewrite, explains the NOT-clause pattern for description fields, advises running trigger evals after each rewrite to measure change, and confirms regression check on existing evals

scenario: User wants to add a hook to a plugin that currently has only skills
expected_behavior: Skill classifies this as medium risk (hook scope creep warning), loads plugin-hooks for event model and matcher patterns, loads plugin-composition for manifest wiring, warns that hooks must be declared in plugin.json and do not auto-discover, and runs full regression check after
